items.summary: On 6 July 2011, José Manuel Barroso, President of the EC, met Muhammad Yunus, 2006 Nobel Peace Prize ® laureate in Strasbourg.

items.title: EU-US leaders meeting and NATO Summit in Warsaw, 08-09/07/2016
items.summary: Poland hosted the North Atlantic Treaty Organisation (NATO) Summit in Warsaw on 8-9 July 2016. Jean-Claude Juncker, President of the EC, Donald Tusk, President of the European Council, and Federica Mogherini, High Representative of the Union for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy and Vice-President of the EC, represented the European Union at the Summit. In the margins of the Summit, President Juncker and President Tusk met Barack Obama, President of the United States, for an EU/US Leaders Meeting. This meeting provided an opportunity to underline Transatlantic unity and to discuss common political, economic and international security challenges.

items.title: Celebration of Slovenia joining the Eurozone: general atmosphere and extracts from speeches
items.summary: Ministers for Finance gave Slovenia the final go-ahead to become the 13th Member of the Eurozone on 1 January 2007. A series of events and celebrations took place in Brussels (rond-point Schuman) to mark the enlargement of the eurozone. An oversized poster, covering part of the Berlaymont building, was unveiled in the presence of the Ministers for Finance of the Eurozone. It depicts both the national side as well as the new common-side of the 1-euro coin. Other elements are planned, like live band and performance of national songs and dances, school children releasing gas balloons in the sky, huge cake carrying the map of the enlarged euro-area, photo shooting and public speeches. This will make Slovenia the first of the EU's 10 new Member States to join the euro zone.

items.title: Economic and Financial Affairs Council: extracts from the press conference on Slovenia joining the Eurozone
items.summary: Ministers for Finance adopted formally two decisions on Slovenia joining the eurozone. Following the recommendations of the European Commission and the European Council, Ministers gave their agreement to Slovenia joining the euro on 1 January 2007 and set fixed, unchangeable conversion rate for the euro and Slovenia's national currency, with a euro expected to be worth 239.64 tolars. The country will become the 13th State introducing the unified EU currency. It will also be the first among the 10 new Member States that joined on 1st May 2004 that will change its national currency. According to Ljubljana’s plan the Slovenians will be able to use at one and the same time the EUR and Tolar for period of two weeks. The prices will be written in two currencies for a period of six months.

items.title: Visit of Ursula von der Leyen, President of the European Commission, to Bosnia and Herzegovina: Visit to Srebrenica
items.summary: On 14 October 2025, Ursula von der Leyen, President of the European Commission, went in Bosnia and Herzegovina, in the context of a visit to Western Balkans countries to reiterated the EU's support for the region's path towards EU accession and gradual access to the Single Market. On this occasion, Ursula von der Leyen met with Bosnia and Herzegovina's Presidency: Željko Komšic, Chairman of the Presidency of Bosnia and Herzegovina, Željka Cvijanović, Chairwoman of the Presidency of Bosnia and Herzegovina, and Denis Bećirović, Chairman of the Presidency of Bosnia and Herzegovina, and with Borjana Krišto, Chairwoman of the Council of Ministers of Bosnia and Herzegovina. Ursula von der Leyen also participated in the 'Critical Voices under Pressure' conference at Europe House and visited the memorial of the victilms of the 1995 genocide in Srebrenica. This video shows extracts of the visit to Srebrenica.

items.title: General views of the Srebrenica Memorial Day
items.summary: On 11 July 2015, international personalities and thousand of people took part to the Srebrenica Memorial Day in Srebrenica-Potocari. They paid tribute to the 8372 victims of the 1995 genocide.

items.title: Western Balkans - 2003
items.summary: The EU is by far the single largest assistance donor to the countries of the Western Balkans. Since 1991, the EU has provided more than 6 billion euro to the region through its various aid programmes. And by 2006, that figure will have risen to some 10 billion euro. The EU leads on the ground, too. In Kosovo, some 36,000 troops (80 per cent of the total force) and 800 civilian police from EU Member States serve alongside other international partners. And the EU is also actively involved in the combined international effort in South-East Europe. All of this represents a significant, long-term commitment on the part of the EU to peace, stability and prosperity in South-East Europe.

events.title: EP Stockshots

items.title: European Automotive Industry's competitiveness (extended version)
items.summary: This infoclip illustrates the automotive industry from different angles, such as car shipping; manufacturing, car dealers, electric vehicles charging, and a car repair garage. The clip shows the automotive port of Antwerp-Bruges, the Audi factory in Brussels and a car dealer in Brussels city center. In spring 2024, the German manufacturer announced the restructuring of its Brussels plant, provoking protest among employees and trade unions. The European automotive sector is experiencing a crisis sparked by the transition to low carbon technologies, the degradation of supply chains, increased competition from Asia, and decreased demand for electric models.

items.title: General views of Dublin (Ireland)
items.summary: This Infoclip captures the cityscape of Dublin, Ireland, showcasing its iconic landmarks and cultural sites, such as Convention Center, Ha'penny Bridge & River Liffey, Samuel Beckett Bridge, Temple Bar District, St. Stephen’s Green, Phoenix Park, Trinity College, The Masonic Square and Compasses, Grafton Street, Áras an Uachtaráin (the official residence of the President of Ireland), Iveagh House (Headquarters of the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade), Ely House and Houses of the Oireachtas (the seat of the Irish Parliament).

items.title: General views of Dublin (Ireland)
items.summary: This infoclip presents general views of Dublin (Ireland), including drone views of the city, Leinster House (Irish Parliament), the Four Courts, the Convention Centre Dublin, the River Liffey and various views of Dublin city streets.

items.title: EP Stockshots
Housing conditions in Vienna (Austria)
items.summary: This infoclip shows what the housing sector in Vienna is like. 76% of the Viennese population is renting. Significant is that 55% of those renting in Vienna are renting a form of limited-profit or public housing. The Limited-Profit Housing Act (Wohnungsgemeinnützigkeitsgesetz, WGG) is a regulation implemented in the 1940s pertaining to cooperative housing associations in Austria. Vienna remains dedicated to affordable housing. Today, the majority of Vienna's population lives in some form of subsidised housing, helping to dampen overall housing prices. An estimated 500 000 people live in municipal housing estates, making the City of Vienna the largest provider of subsidised housing in Europe. However, over the past decade, housing affordability in Austria has decreased and the average household is spending an ever higher share of their income on housing.

items.title: EU Cybersecurity
items.summary: This news item shows various elements, including cutaways of antennas and security cameras, illustration of a cybercriminal in action and a server under cyber attack. It also features the Ubitech co-founders highlighting key innovations like a quantum computer model, an AI server, and the MAI.gov government platform for providing online services to citizens. The video further includes illustrations of devices such as smartwatches, smart lights, and security cameras, as well as shots of the European Union agency ENISA's headquarters, dedicated to strengthening cybersecurity, and scenes from the European Cyber Security Challenge competition in Turin.

items.title: EU Cyber Resilience Act, better protection for smart devices
items.summary: The infoclips illustrates the devices which fall under the requirements of the Cyber Resilience Act, like smart vacuum cleaners, smart washing machine, smart watches, security cameras, etc. The latest proposal in the EU Cyber Resilience Act Introduces mandatory cybersecurity requirements for products with digital elements and it covers a broad range of devices - it would include all products that are connected either directly or indirectly to another device or network, including hardware, software and ancillary services and would impose obligations on manufacturers, importers, and distributors of these products to provide duty of care across their whole life cycle. The proposal aims to ensure better protection for consumers through increasing the responsibility of manufacturers by obliging them to provide security support and software updates to address identified vulnerabilities, and providing them with information about cybersecurity of products they buy and use.

items.title: Unwavering support to Ukraine
items.summary: This Infoclip focuses on EU–Ukraine relations, presenting footage of President von der Leyen’s preparatory meeting at the Ukrainian Embassy in the United States, as well as images from Ukraine of rescue services and daily life. It features European Parliament President Roberta Metsola’s visit with Ruslan Stefanchuk to a bombed school near Kyiv, her meeting with President Zelenskyy in Brussels, and extracts from Zelenskyy’s intervention at a European Council. Additional material shows a formal sitting with Stefanchuk in Strasbourg, the European Parliament illuminated in the Ukranian colours, and the Ukranian Independence Day ceremony in Brussels. On the occasion of the State of the European Union (SOTEU) debate Commission President von der Leyen outlines her priorities for the year ahead, addressing key European themes, including the EU's unwavering support to Ukraine.

items.title: Kyiv (Ukraine) - General views of the city and consequences of the Russian attacks
items.summary: This infoclip shows general views of Kyiv, including daily street life, traffic, and key landmarks. It documents the visible consequences of Russian attacks on residential buildings and other civilian infrastructure. The footage also features monuments and memorials dedicated to the victims of the war. The shooting took place on 22/07/2025.

items.title: Western Balkans and the EU
items.summary: The European Union has close links with the Western Balkans partners, aiming to secure stable, prosperous and well-functioning democratic societies on a steady path towards the EU. The cooperation between the EU and the Western Balkans aims at effective political and economic governance, the highest standards of adherence to the rule of law, promoting media freedom and a vibrant civil society. This infoclip shows general views of the cities of Tirana (Albania), Podgorica (Montenegro), Pristina (Kosovo), Sarajevo (Bosnia and Herzegovina) and Skopje (North Macedonia).

items.title: Chisinau (Moldova), city views ahead of the upcoming Parliamentary Elections
items.summary: This infoclip shows city views of Chisinau ahead of the upcoming parliamentary elections in Moldova. It includes exterior views of the Casa Guvernului, the official seat of the Government of Moldova, the Parlamentul Republicii Moldova, a polling station, and the City Council. Additional footage focuses on the electoral campaign in the capital, with political party posters displayed across the city, gazebos set up by parties to present their programmes, and campaigners actively canvassing by handing out flyers to citizens. The sequence concludes with general cutaway shots from Chisinau.

events.title: EP Stockshots

items.title: Wildfires in Europe
items.summary: This infoclip shows images of wildfires, firefighters extinguishing fires, evacuations of the population in Spain and Italy. In 2025, the wildfire season has been well above average, with a cumulative burnt area in the European Union of more than 1 million hectares, according to the European Forest Fire Information System (EFFIS). It’s the highest value ever recorded inside the European Union since 2006. Between 8 and 19 August, the estimated total burnt area in both Portugal and Spain was over 500,000 hectares, according to EFFIS, making it the countries’ highest value of burnt areas ever recorded in mid-August, comparable to almost twice the size of Luxembourg. © Images of firefighters battling fires in Spain are strictly for journalistic purposes. For any other use, please contact the press office of the Spanish Emergency Military Unit (Unidad Militar de Emergencias).

items.title: Heatwave
items.summary: This infoclip illustrates how the heatwaves impact the daily-life of Europeans. It stages people hiding in the shade in a public park, eating ice-creams, buying bottles of water, sipping cold-drinks and playing with water fountains on a public place.
